# Questions in Livestream 253

Live Stream #253: The Grassy Nome Theory

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=556" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:16</a> | Is it sinful to abuse the `weaker brother` idea by trying to hold other people's private behavior hostage? | Yes. He said flaunting freedom can be sinful in public, but using that text to control someone else's private conduct is also a misuse of the passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=697" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:37</a> | Is there evidence for a 40-year desolation of Egypt under Nebuchadnezzar in Ezekiel 29? | He said there is some evidence of Babylonian looting and disruption in Egypt's heartland, but not strong evidence for a literal total desolation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:30</a> | Who was the woman scholar mentioned for prehistoric Anatolia? | He clarified that he meant Erica Hughes as a specialist in prehistoric Anatolia, not an antediluvian or pre-flood scholar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=1030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:10</a> | Why do some early-date Exodus proponents say the previous pharaoh must have reigned more than 40 years? | He said the argument depends on combining Acts 7 with Exodus 4, but he rejected it because Exodus 4:19 says all the men seeking Moses' life were dead, not one pharaoh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=1648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:28</a> | Who were the `Philistines` in Abraham and Isaac's time? | He treated that label as a retrojection for later readers and thought the people in view were Canaanites on the coastal plain, not actual later Philistines or `Paleo-Hyksos`. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=1745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:05</a> | Why do the borders in Genesis 15 seem much larger than later Israelite territory? | He said the promise is to Abraham's descendants broadly, not only to Israel, so it includes other related peoples spread across a much wider region. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=1890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:30</a> | Did Solomon's kingdom extend to most of the Genesis 15 territory? | Not most of it. He said Solomon's kingdom reached up toward the Golan Heights and a little into Syria, but did not cover the whole promised range. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=1997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:17</a> | What are your thoughts on the Kalašma language tablet? | He said only that it appears to reflect a Luwian dialect related to Hittite and did not claim detailed familiarity with it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=2135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:35</a> | May Christians eat foods such as pork and shrimp? | Yes. He treated the dietary restrictions as part of the fulfilled ritual law rather than binding on Christians. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=2304" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:24</a> | What do you think of Graeme Hancock, Mark Armitage, and `big archaeology` conspiracy claims? | He rejected the conspiracy framing, arguing archaeology is underfunded and that such claims confuse fields and ignore the actual evidence problems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=2843" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:23</a> | What happens to Christians who die before Christ's return? | He said believers go directly to be with the Lord in heaven before the final resurrection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=2940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:00</a> | Do Christians pray in heaven and in the new heavens and new earth? | He said prayer as a means of conforming our will becomes unnecessary in heaven because there is direct access to God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=3230" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:50</a> | Will people need to cover their eyes before God like angels around the throne? | He said probably not, treating the angelic wing-covering more as modesty language than as a response to personal sinfulness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=3439" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:19</a> | Do Jewish Christians still need to keep the ritual law? | No. He said the ritual law is fulfilled in Christ, even if some Jewish believers still choose to observe parts of it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=3535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:55</a> | Have any Revelation events happened in this century? | He said not so far. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=3581" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:41</a> | Will pets and other animals go to heaven? | He tentatively thought some animals do, while qualifying that not all animals necessarily share the same destiny or relationship to humans there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=3835" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:55</a> | Is the story in 1 Kings 3:16-28 original to Kings? | He said it is original to Kings, while the harder question is whether it goes back to Solomon himself; he leaned toward yes because it fits Solomon's reputation for wisdom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=3992" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:32</a> | Would anyone actually react by saying to divide the child in Solomon's judgment? | Yes. He said the reaction is consistent with human spite and shows which claimant does not truly care for the child. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=4131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:51</a> | Do we know anything about the wise men named in 1 Kings 4:31? | Not really. He said they seem to have been known wisdom figures around Solomon's time, but no independent records of them are known to him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=4255" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:55</a> | In 1 Kings 6:18, was it important that no stone could be seen in the temple? | He said the stone was paneled with cedar, carved, and overlaid with gold, likely to achieve ornate relief work the local stone could not provide well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=4411" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:31</a> | Will a third temple be built, and must the Dome of the Rock fall first? | He thought a third temple will be built, but said the Dome of the Rock need not be removed because he locates the relevant temple site near the Dome of the Tablets. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=4672" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:52</a> | In 1 Kings 6:20, was it acceptable for Solomon to alter the altar? | Yes. He said cedar and gold are ritually pure, and Solomon needed new temple furnishings anyway. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=4740" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:00</a> | Does 1 Kings 8:8 show that this section was written before the exile? | He said it may preserve a pre-exilic source, but Kings as a compiled book was collated during the exile from earlier materials. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=4847" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:47</a> | Was Pharaoh's daughter Solomon's great queen? | Not as far as he knows. He said Israelite succession was not organized around a great-queen system the way Egyptian succession could be. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=5030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:50</a> | How common was queen-mother authority, and does it translate to Mary in Christ's kingdom? | Queen mothers commonly had some authority, but he warned against using that political pattern to give Mary authority over Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=5246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:26</a> | Is Matthew 12:48-50 relevant to claims about Mary's authority? | Yes. He said Jesus' statement about his mother and brothers shows obedience to the Father, not biological relation, as the controlling issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=5365" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:25</a> | Does 1 Kings show that Egypt, Moses, and the Exodus were already cemented in Israel's memory? | Yes. He said Kings and the prophets already treat the Exodus as central to Israel's national identity, against claims that the Pentateuch came only later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=5450" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:50</a> | Why did Hiram pay Solomon in 1 Kings 9:14? | He treated it as a toll or tariff for access through Solomon's territory, especially for trade routes toward Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=5603" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:23</a> | Do we know which pharaoh took Gezer in 1 Kings 9:16? | He said Siamun is the most likely pharaoh in view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=5716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:16</a> | Does 1 Kings 9:21 prove the beginning of Kings was written before the Babylonian exile? | Not the whole book. He said it shows Kings is copying pre-exilic source material, even though the final compilation is post-exilic or exilic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=5904" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:24</a> | What is the Millo in 1 Kings 9:24? | He identified the Millo as a defensive wall and leaned toward Eilat Mazar's case for locating it near the City of David palace area. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=6059" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:59</a> | Did Samson carry the city gate doors in Judges 16:3? | Yes, he thought the text implies Samson ripped off the doors with the posts and bars and carried them away. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=6256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:16</a> | Is the amount of gold in 1 Kings 10:14 possible? | Yes. He said ancient rulers accumulated large amounts of gold and compared the figure with the gold in Tutankhamun's tomb. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=6631" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:31</a> | How did Tutankhamun's tomb escape being fully raided before discovery? | He said it had been raided once, but a later work village covered the entrance and caused its location to be forgotten. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=7408" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:28</a> | How come Nimrod was not against God? | He said Nimrod was subduing wild land and beasts, which fits the creation mandate rather than opposing God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=7576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:16</a> | Was aversion to the material world common in Greco-Roman culture or unique to later Gnostic sects? | He said the idea has roots in Neoplatonism, but Gnostic religion drove it to a stronger anti-material conclusion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=7814" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:14</a> | Does Exodus 3:14 in the Septuagint connect with Plato's concept of the One? | He did not think so, treating the Septuagint wording as translation rather than a direct Platonic borrowing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=8831" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:11</a> | How do you determine the genre of a biblical text? | He said to read widely across ancient genres and attend to textual clues such as poetry, war chronicle language, prophetic material, apocalyptic imagery, or dedicatory inscriptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=9129" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:09</a> | Did Tertullian become and remain excommunicated? | He said Tertullian's excommunication is complicated and argued it happened before Montanism became the later explanation attached to it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=9569" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:29</a> | Could a settler population that replaces a native population change the cosmic ruler or prince of a country? | Possibly. He said either the cosmic ruler changes or switches to the new population, but the criteria are not clear enough to say more. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=9779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:59</a> | Where do you think the Garden of Eden was? | He located it in the ancient Sealand area near the Mesopotamian river mouths, now largely underwater. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=9932" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:32</a> | When did ancient Israelites name their children? | He said the naming was generally done on the eighth day, tied to circumcision for boys and a naming rite for girls. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=10379" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:59</a> | Can you elaborate on being poor in spirit? | He described it as not flaunting spiritual status or strength, but approaching God with humility and dependence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=10565" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:05</a> | How do you interpret 1 Corinthians 14:15, and is it related to hair and reproduction beliefs? | He said no; he treated the passage as about modesty and ordered worship rather than a reproduction theory of hair. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=10996" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:16</a> | Did ancient Israelites or Second Temple Jews know of a third power in heaven, the Holy Spirit? | Yes. He said they knew of the Holy Spirit as a third power, though later doctrine explains that more fully. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=11150" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:50</a> | Is it a fallacy to take first attestation as the beginning of existence? | Yes. He said first attestation only marks the earliest surviving evidence, not the actual beginning of a place, people, or practice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=11522" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:02</a> | Have you visited the Israel Museum, and what are your thoughts? | No. He said he has never been to Israel or visited the museum, though he is familiar with some of its holdings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/_6WmbdDHC8s?t=12104" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:44</a> | Are you familiar with temple scholars Joseph Good and Rico Cortes? | No. He said they are outside his usual sphere of study and influence. |
